concerns.text: Task T007 implements 'quantization_emulator.py' using 'torch.quantize_per_tensor' (deterministic), but Task T012 (US1) requires 'stochastic rounding' to satisfy FR-001/FR-009. The foundational task (T007) does not produce the artifact (stochastic noise) required by the consumer task (T012). T007 must be revised to implement stochastic rounding logic before T012 can consume it.

Task T005 sets up 'data/loader.py' to stream Kinetics-400 with 'no synthetic fallback'. Task T013 integrates this loader. However, the 'plan.md' mentions a UCF101 fallback which is absent in T005. If Kinetics-400 fails, T013 will fail immediately. The foundational task T005 must include the fallback logic (or a clear error path) before T013 can safely consume it in a CI environment.

Task T009 sets up 'clip_evaluator.py' as a frozen model. Task T021 integrates it with the training loop. However, Task T022 (US2) requires validation against 'synthetic ground truth (frame swaps/cuts)' to satisfy FR-007 (which spec says requires human labels, but plan says synthetic). The foundational setup T009 does not include the logic to generate or handle these synthetic discontinuities. T009 or a new foundational task must produce the 'discontinuity generator' artifact before T022 can consume it.

Task T029 (US3) requires 'non-linear regression' to identify a threshold. This depends on the 'precision-consistency curve' data. However, the foundational task T007 (quantization emulator) is currently defined as deterministic. If T007 does not produce the stochastic noise required for the 'degradation' curve, T029 will have no data to regress. The producer (T007) must be corrected to produce stochastic data before the consumer (T029) can function.

Tasks T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and placed before T012 (implementation). While tests can be written in parallel, the description 'Unit test for stochastic rounding' (T010) implies the existence of the stochastic logic in T007. If T007 is not yet implemented with stochastic logic, T010 cannot be written to 'FAIL' as required by the 'Tests' section note. The ordering implies T010 depends on T007's specific implementation details, making the [P] tag potentially unsafe if T007 is incomplete.
